title: rubus insericatus subsp. newbouldianus
intro: Rubus insericatus subsp. newbouldianus, also known as Newbould's black raspberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, Rubusaceae. It is native to the UK and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, arthritis, and skin conditions, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. Herbal preparations of Newbould's black raspberry include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
